meaning: adj.巨大的,庞大的adv.非常地,异常地
examples: In Club Penguin, the market leader, which was bought by Disney in 2007 for a whopping $700 million.
Inflation remains high at a whopping 17%.
Data traffic thus grew by a whopping 158%.
Danhi and colleagues go through a whopping six to eight pork shoulders per day.
One advisor told Inveen that a whopping 80% of his clients were not profitable.
